zotero.abstractNote: Simulation-based inference (SBI) aims to perform Bayesian inference on parameters of complex simulators where the likelihood is intractable. While traditional SBI methods operate on finite-dimensional parameter spaces, many scientific problems involve inferring latent functions, such as fields or processes. Current approaches for function-space inference often rely on discretization, which can lead to high-dimensional parameter spaces and poor scaling with resolution. We introduce FNOPE (Fourier Neural Operator Posterior Estimation), a novel framework for SBI on function spaces. FNOPE leverages the resolution-invariance and efficient spectral representation of Fourier Neural Operators to learn posterior distributions over function spaces directly. We demonstrate that FNOPE achieves state-of-the-art performance on several challenging benchmarks, including inferring latent fields in partial differential equations and stochastic processes, while maintaining computational efficiency and resolution-invariance.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 38 (NeurIPS 2025)
zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 38 (NeurIPS 2025)
